mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and expanding economic activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales, indicating strong consumer demand, signals improving corporate revenues and profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market participants react quickly.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth exceeds 0.8% month-over-month, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns fail to be positive in at least 70% of occurrences over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer prices and declining industrial production indicates a stagflationary environment, where inflation erodes purchasing power and production slows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon due to reduced earnings and higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3% AND INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% YoY and INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% MoM, yet S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery, strong GDP growth signals a broad economic rebound, boosting investor confidence and corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery periods, if GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% QoQ annualized, but the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate, especially from a low base, signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by 0.5 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over any 3-month period, and the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, stimulating economic activity and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 12 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns show a negative average or are positive in less than 50% of observations during this period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with robust retail sales indicate broad-based strength in both investment and consumer demand, signaling a healthy economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200 (thousands of units) AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200k units and RSAFS growth is above 0.5% MoM, if the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of such occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production, particularly when the economy is emerging from a slowdown, indicates a rebound in manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which typically precedes stronger corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.7% after a period of decline
failure_condition: In an early-cycle recovery, if INDPRO growth exceeds 0.7% MoM, but the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.
